release_gate.evidence: checked-in normalization audit and executable high-nu run plan, not completed high-nu scan evidence

claims: High-collisionality transport-matrix trends should agree with analytic asymptotic limits
This full analytic-limit reproduction is closed as a post-release research lane until a wider high-nu scan is available; a bounded normalization audit is now checked in

release_gate.evidence: checked-in high-collisionality tail-slope proxy summary and figures
release_gate.promotion_gate: Do not relabel as the full analytic-limit reproduction until the parent high-nu scan gate is closed

claims: PAS L11/L12 high-collisionality tails have positive power-law slopes on both corrected full collisionality artifacts
W7-X Fokker-Planck L11/L12 tails show inverse-nu-like slopes on the checked-in nu'<=10 artifact

LHD Fokker-Planck L11/L12 tails do not yet reach the asymptotic inverse-nu gate on the checked-in scan, so this implemented artifact is a bounded trend proxy rather than the full analytic-limit reproduction
acceptance_gates: Summary JSON records the high-collisionality tail slopes and source artifacts
PAS L11/L12 slopes are positive on both LHD and W7-X corrected full scans
The artifact must explicitly mark LHD as needing a wider high-nu scan before replacing the analytic-limit reproduction

release_gate.evidence: checked-in Appendix-B geometry and normalization audit summary
release_gate.promotion_gate: Keep ready_for_literature_claim false until both LHD and W7-X collisionality scans extend to the configured high-nu threshold

claims: The checked-in LHD and W7-X output files contain the Appendix-B geometry ingredients needed for a reproducible normalization audit
The current full collisionality summaries stop near nu'=10, so the audit keeps the full Simakov-Helander analytic-limit reproduction closed until wider high-nu scans are pinned
W7-X FP L11/L12 tails satisfy the inverse-nu slope proxy on the current range, while LHD still fails that gate
acceptance_gates: Summary JSON records Appendix-B geometry quantities, FSABHat2 recomputation error, tail slopes, and full-overlay readiness gates
The full analytic-limit gate remains false unless both cases extend to the configured high-nu threshold
The figure must show the inverse-nu tail proxy, slope-window sensitivity, and explicit readiness table

All trajectory models agree at Er=0 within the strict pinned tolerance

release_gate.evidence: checked-in CPU/GPU 39-case frozen suite benchmark summary and figures
release_gate.promotion_gate: Require 39/39 parity_ok on CPU and GPU, zero strict mismatches, zero jax_error, and zero max_attempts before regenerating public benchmark figures

claims: The release-facing frozen CPU suite has 39/39 parity_ok cases with no strict mismatches
The release-facing frozen GPU suite has 39/39 parity_ok cases with no strict mismatches

Runtime, maximum-RSS, and cold/warm bars and ratios are generated from checked-in suite reports, with top offenders, warm timing-source counts, and excluded sub-10-second reference rows recorded in machine-readable JSON
Public runtime/memory plots filter to rows whose SFINCS Fortran v3 reference runtime is at least 10 seconds; shorter rows remain CI parity checks unless rerun at production-comparison resolution
acceptance_gates: CPU and GPU frozen reports each contain all 39 audited example-suite cases
Every audited case is parity_ok with zero jax_error, zero max_attempts, and zero strict mismatches
Runtime, memory, cold/warm, and ratio data are recomputed from the frozen reports before plotting, not copied into the figure by hand
The README-facing benchmark summary records the 10-second Fortran runtime threshold and the excluded short-reference rows
